_meta.description: Curated scholar-led Quran learning tracks. Each track is a sequence of stages a learner completes at their own pace. Stages reference verified Quran verses, hadith, and tafsir/book sources; no AI-generated content. Sources align with Athari/Salafi-compatible classical works (per PDF Section 8) without sectarian framing.

tracks.title: Foundations of Tawhid
tracks.subtitle: The bedrock of belief through Quran and the Prophet's example
tracks.intro: Tawhid is the heart of Islam. This 14-stage track grounds you in the Quranic foundations of monotheism through verses, hadith, and short tafsir excerpts. Each stage takes 10–20 minutes.

stages.title: The opening declaration
stages.summary: Surah Al-Ikhlas — the most concise statement of Allah's oneness.
stages.reflection_prompt: What does it mean for Allah to be 'as-Samad' (the Self-Sufficient) in your daily life?

stages.title: Allah's oneness in lordship
stages.summary: Tawhid ar-Rububiyyah — Allah alone is Creator, Sustainer, and Disposer.
stages.reflection_prompt: Name three signs in your day that point to Allah's lordship over creation.

stages.title: Allah's oneness in worship
stages.summary: Tawhid al-Uluhiyyah — worship belongs to Allah alone.
stages.reflection_prompt: Audit one act of worship today: is it purely for Allah?

stages.title: His most beautiful names
stages.summary: Tawhid al-Asma wa-l-Sifat — affirming Allah's names without distortion.
stages.reflection_prompt: Pick one of Allah's names from Surah Al-Hashr 59:22–24 and reflect on its impact.

stages.title: Ayat al-Kursi
stages.summary: The greatest verse — a complete summary of Allah's attributes.
stages.reflection_prompt: Commit Ayat al-Kursi to memory if you haven't yet, and recite it each morning.

stages.title: The trap of shirk
stages.summary: Recognising and avoiding the major and minor forms of associating partners with Allah.
stages.reflection_prompt: Reflect on the difference between major and minor shirk. Which subtle forms might affect you?

stages.title: Sincerity of intention
stages.summary: Ikhlas — the inner condition that gives worship its weight.
stages.reflection_prompt: Identify one good deed you do habitually. For whom is it really being done?

stages.title: Trust in Allah
stages.summary: Tawakkul — acting with means while relying ultimately on Allah.
stages.reflection_prompt: Where in your life are you trusting your own planning more than Allah?

stages.title: Fear, hope, and love
stages.summary: The three pillars of the believer's relationship with Allah.
stages.reflection_prompt: Are you currently leaning more on fear, hope, or love? What's the right balance?

stages.title: Salah as the proof of tawhid
stages.summary: Why the daily prayer is the most concrete enactment of belief.
stages.reflection_prompt: Pick one prayer today and pray it as if it were your last.

stages.title: The signs in creation
stages.summary: How Allah points to His oneness through the natural world.
stages.reflection_prompt: Step outside today and observe one ayah (sign) of Allah's creation.

stages.title: The end-state of the believer
stages.summary: What tawhid promises in this life and the next.
stages.reflection_prompt: How does the certainty of meeting Allah re-order your priorities this week?

stages.title: Seeking refuge
stages.summary: Surah Al-Falaq and An-Nas — the Prophet's protection against external and internal harm.
stages.reflection_prompt: Add the Mu'awwidhatayn to your morning and evening adhkar.

stages.title: Carrying tawhid forward
stages.summary: Closing reflection — how to keep tawhid alive after this track ends.
stages.reflection_prompt: Write down three habits you'll keep to preserve what you've learned.

tracks.title: Patience and Perseverance
tracks.subtitle: Sabr through Quranic stories and prophetic example
tracks.intro: A 7-stage track on sabr — patience with obedience, against sin, and through hardship — grounded in Quran narratives.

stages.title: What sabr is
stages.summary: Surah Al-Asr — patience as one of the four conditions for salvation.
stages.reflection_prompt: Of the four mentioned in Surah Al-Asr, which is hardest for you?

stages.title: Yusuf in the well
stages.summary: Patience in trial without bitterness.
stages.reflection_prompt: Notice Ya'qub's wording: 'beautiful patience'. What makes patience beautiful?

stages.title: Ayyub's affliction
stages.summary: Trial of body and family — supplication without complaint.
stages.reflection_prompt: What is the difference between expressing pain to Allah and complaining about Him?

stages.title: Reward of the patient
stages.summary: Patience rewarded without measure.
stages.reflection_prompt: Name one trial in your life. What hidden reward might be on its other side?

stages.title: Patience with people
stages.summary: Dealing with criticism, family disputes, and disagreement.
stages.reflection_prompt: Identify one relationship that needs more patience from you. What's the next small step?

stages.title: Patience as worship
stages.summary: Steadfastness in salah and obedience.
stages.reflection_prompt: Which act of worship is hardest for you to maintain? Make a small commitment for the next 7 days.

stages.title: Closing — carrying sabr forward
stages.summary: Reflection on what you've internalised.
stages.reflection_prompt: Re-read Surah Al-Asr from stage 1. What has changed in how you hear it now?

tracks.title: A Day with the Quran
tracks.subtitle: Anchoring your daily rhythm to revealed words
tracks.intro: A 5-stage walk through the Quranic and prophetic shape of a believer's day — morning, midday, afternoon, evening, and night.

stages.title: Beginning the day
stages.summary: Morning adhkar and the first thoughts.
stages.reflection_prompt: What is the first sentence you say after waking? Could it be a remembrance instead?

stages.title: Mid-morning — work as worship
stages.summary: Provision is sought, not invented.
stages.reflection_prompt: How does seeing work as worship change one task on your list today?

stages.title: Midday — Dhuhr and rest
stages.summary: Pausing in the middle of effort.
stages.reflection_prompt: Are you reaching Dhuhr depleted? What 5 minutes of slowing down could prepare you for it?

stages.title: Evening — Asr and gratitude
stages.summary: Surah Al-Asr again — noticing time.
stages.reflection_prompt: Name three blessings from today before Maghrib.

stages.title: Night — closing the day
stages.summary: The last words before sleep — forgiveness and trust.
stages.reflection_prompt: What three things in your day need forgiveness? Ask now.
